role_summary: SNCC Mississippi voter-registration cohort member; in this corpus's embedding her testimony pairs strongly with Charles McLaurin, Frankye Adams-Johnson, Euvester Simpson, and the Ladner sisters.
ai_reading: Henderson's top semantic neighbors are Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 183, the Sunflower County project director), Frankye Adams-Johnson (entry 43), Euvester Simpson (entry 41), the Ladner sisters joint interview (entry 75), and Peggy Jean Connor (entry 96). All five are Mississippi SNCC voter-registration figures; the embedding places Henderson cleanly inside that cohort, the tightest Mississippi grouping she belongs to in the catalog. Her +0.49 Sacred Framing reading is consistent with the Black Mississippi church-based grassroots organizing of the period; her +0.51 Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision reading adds to the cluster's strategic-side pull (Simpson, McLaurin, and Adams-Johnson also land on the strategic side), reinforcing the catalog's observation that the Mississippi voter-registration cohort's oral histories read as strategically-framed despite the action-on-the-ground specificity of the work itself.
biographical_paragraph: Juadine Henderson's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 68 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
